positiveClaims: The existing Google Docs evidence claim gate is product-bound as a Review Bridge claim boundary.

A Google Docs evidence claim can be accepted only when a valid Google Docs evidence packet is present, the evidence hash matches, requested coverage is not wider than packet coverage, and required docsSuggestions and driveComments evidence classes are covered.
Accepted Google Docs evidence packet classes are docsSuggestions, driveComments, and structuralManual.
Accepted Google Docs evidence claims require docsSuggestions and driveComments coverage; structuralManual alone is not sufficient for claim acceptance.
Missing evidence packet, invalid evidence packet, invalid claim, hash mismatch, coverage-exceeded cases, and missing required docsSuggestions or driveComments classes block the claim.
The binding is evidence and claim truth only; it is not content import, review import, apply, export, storage truth, Google API integration, or Google Drive integration.
nonClaims: No Google Docs support is claimed.
No Google Docs import is claimed.
No Google Docs review import is claimed.
No Google Docs sync is claimed.
No Google Docs roundtrip is claimed.
No Google Docs layout parity is claimed.
No full Google Docs fidelity is claimed.
No full Google Docs review import is claimed.
No Google API or Google Drive integration is claimed.
No network, OAuth, account, cloud, or live Google service access is claimed.
No exact apply from Google Docs is claimed.
No structural apply is claimed.
No comment auto-apply is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by Google Docs evidence claim binding.
No project truth write is performed by Google Docs evidence claim binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by Google Docs evidence claim binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No release readiness is claimed.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Evidence layer validates packet and claim only.
Claim binding is not a parser and does not inspect Google Docs files.
Claim binding is not content import and does not create scenes.
Claim binding is not Review import and does not activate a Review session.
Claim binding is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
Claim binding is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
Claim binding is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Claim binding is not a Google API integration and does not perform network access.
Review Bridge may reference accepted claims as bounded evidence, but accepted claims do not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test Google Docs and Word evidence claim binding targeted contracts
node --test release claim adjacent boundary contracts

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed the next contour is Google Docs evidence claim binding; flagged missing status artifact and forbidden broad support or import claims before this diff created the status and nonclaim fence.

Confirmed the existing Google Docs evidence kernel already exports schemas, packet classes, reason codes, hash, and claim gate; runtime gap is bookkeeping only, with hygiene scoped to the Google Docs evidence contract.

nextStep: continue with the next owner-selected Review Bridge claim boundary
